Pratt & Whitney and GE Set to Assemble Next-Gen Adaptive Cycle Engines
articles.summary: Pratt & Whitney and GE Aerospace have completed the Assembly Readiness Reviews for their XA103 and XA102 adaptive cycle engines, respectively, marking a transition from digital models to physical assembly for testing. These engines, part of the Next Generation Adaptive Propulsion (NGAP) program, are intended to power future U.S. Air Force aircraft like the F-47 NGAD fighter, with testing expected in the late 2020s. A $3.5 billion contract has been awarded to both companies for prototype development, though the engines might not be ready for the F-47's initial flight by 2029.
articles.content: Pratt & Whitney and GE Aerospace have completed the Assembly Readiness Reviews for their XA103 and XA102 adaptive cycle engines, respectively, marking a transition from digital models to physical assembly for testing. These engines, part of the Next Generation Adaptive Propulsion (NGAP) program, are intended to power future U.S. Air Force aircraft like the F-47 NGAD fighter, with testing expected in the late 2020s. A $3.5 billion contract has been awarded to both companies for prototype development, though the engines might not be ready for the F-47's initial flight by 2029.

articles.title: BrainsWay stock falls over 2% despite beating Q1 estimates on guidance
articles.summary: BrainsWay Ltd. (NASDAQ:BWAY) reported strong Q1 2026 results, surpassing analyst estimates with adjusted EPS of $0.06 and revenue growth of 35% year-over-year to $15.5 million. Despite beating expectations and shipping a record number of Deep TMS systems, the company's stock fell over 2% in pre-market trading as investors reacted to its reiterated full-year guidance, which remained largely in line with analyst consensus. BrainsWay also highlighted significant progress in expanding Deep TMS awareness, broader reimbursement, and secured the first insurer coverage for its accelerated SWIFT Deep T
articles.content: BrainsWay Ltd. (NASDAQ:BWAY) reported strong Q1 2026 results, surpassing analyst estimates with adjusted EPS of $0.06 and revenue growth of 35% year-over-year to $15.5 million. Despite beating expectations and shipping a record number of Deep TMS systems, the company's stock fell over 2% in pre-market trading as investors reacted to its reiterated full-year guidance, which remained largely in line with analyst consensus. BrainsWay also highlighted significant progress in expanding Deep TMS awareness, broader reimbursement, and secured the first insurer coverage for its accelerated SWIFT Deep TMS protocol.

articles.title: CDW (NASDAQ: CDW) adds $1B to stock buyback authorization
articles.summary: CDW Corporation has announced a $1 billion increase to its share repurchase program, augmenting the existing $484 million remaining from a prior authorization. This move provides CDW with substantial capacity to return cash to stockholders and is part of its ongoing capital allocation strategy, which includes dividends and strategic M&A. Since its IPO in 2013, the company has returned approximately $8.4 billion to stockholders.
articles.content: CDW Corporation has announced a $1 billion increase to its share repurchase program, augmenting the existing $484 million remaining from a prior authorization. Since its IPO in 2013, the company has returned approximately $8.4 billion to stockholders.

articles.title: eBay said no to GameStop. Preloved Fashion May Have Dodged A Bullet.
articles.summary: eBay Inc. has rejected GameStop Corp.'s unsolicited $56 billion offer to acquire the e-commerce firm. eBay cited "the uncertainty" around financing and the proposal's impact on its future. This decision means that the preloved fashion market, particularly platforms like Depop recently acquired by eBay, will not fall under the control of a gaming retailer, a move experts believe avoids a steep learning curve for GameStop given the vast differences in their respective secondhand markets.
articles.content: eBay Inc. has rejected GameStop Corp.'s unsolicited $56 billion offer to acquire the e-commerce firm. eBay cited "the uncertainty" around financing and the proposal's impact on its future. This decision means that the preloved fashion market, particularly platforms like Depop recently acquired by eBay, will not fall under the control of a gaming retailer, a move experts believe avoids a steep learning curve for GameStop given the vast differences in their respective secondhand markets.

Corporación América Airports Reports First Quarter 2026 Results
articles.summary: Corporación América Airports (CAAP) announced strong first quarter 2026 results, with consolidated revenues ex-IFRIC12 reaching $495.2 million, an 18.8% year-over-year increase. The company saw a 7.0% increase in passenger traffic and a 26.1% rise in Adjusted EBITDA ex-IFRIC12 to $196.2 million. CEO Martín Eurnekian highlighted broad-based growth across their airport network and continued progress on financial metrics, with robust liquidity and strategic advancements in concession agreements.
articles.content: Corporación América Airports (CAAP) announced strong first quarter 2026 results, with consolidated revenues ex-IFRIC12 reaching $495.2 million, an 18.8% year-over-year increase. The company saw a 7.0% increase in passenger traffic and a 26.1% rise in Adjusted EBITDA ex-IFRIC12 to $196.2 million. CEO Martín Eurnekian highlighted broad-based growth across their airport network and continued progress on financial metrics, with robust liquidity and strategic advancements in concession agreements.

articles.title: Procter & Gamble makes performance gains with migration of SAP systems to Microsoft Azure
articles.summary: Procter & Gamble (P&G) has achieved significant performance improvements and strengthened cyber resiliency by migrating its SAP systems to Microsoft Azure in a multiyear transformation program called 'Phoenix'. The migration led to faster financial close cycles, quicker backend processing, and improved disaster recovery capabilities, cutting recovery time for critical workloads by half. P&G also established a standardized global platform for SAP infrastructure, allowing for consistent security and operational practices across regions.
articles.content: Procter & Gamble (P&G) has achieved significant performance improvements and strengthened cyber resiliency by migrating its SAP systems to Microsoft Azure in a multiyear transformation program called 'Phoenix'. The migration led to faster financial close cycles, quicker backend processing, and improved disaster recovery capabilities, cutting recovery time for critical workloads by half. P&G also established a standardized global platform for SAP infrastructure, allowing for consistent security and operational practices across regions.

Management, including CEO Ari Kahn and CFO Thomas Windhausen, will host a live conference call and webcast at 4:30 p.m. ET to discuss the results. articles.title: Corporación América Airports (NYSE: CAAP) posts strong Q1 2026 profit
articles.summary: Corporación América Airports (CAAP) reported a strong Q1 2026 with revenue up 20.1% to $537.6 million and net income doubling to $80.4 million. Passenger traffic increased by 7.0%, driven by international and transit growth across its airports in Argentina, Brazil, Armenia, Uruguay, and Ecuador. The company maintained a robust financial position with $666.2 million in cash and cash equivalents and an improved net debt to LTM Adjusted EBITDA ratio of 0.5x, reflecting solid operating performance and disciplined capital allocation.
articles.content: Corporación América Airports (CAAP) reported a strong Q1 2026 with revenue up 20.1% to $537.6 million and net income doubling to $80.4 million. Passenger traffic increased by 7.0%, driven by international and transit growth across its airports in Argentina, Brazil, Armenia, Uruguay, and Ecuador. The company maintained a robust financial position with $666.2 million in cash and cash equivalents and an improved net debt to LTM Adjusted EBITDA ratio of 0.5x, reflecting solid operating performance and disciplined capital allocation.
